institutes.countryIntroGermany: Germany is home to world-renowned robotics research institutions, from the DLR and Fraunhofer institutes to leading university labs pioneering humanoids, industrial automation, and AI-driven systems.
institutes.countryIntroAustria: Austria's robotics research landscape features innovative labs and centers focused on autonomous systems, human-robot interaction, and industrial automation.
institutes.countryIntroSwitzerland: Switzerland hosts some of the world's most prestigious robotics labs, including ETH Zurich's institutes and EPFL's research centers, advancing legged robots, drones, and soft robotics.

meta.title: MegaRobotics | Industrial Robotics & Automation Solutions
meta.description: MegaRobotics helps European industrial customers and research institutions identify, evaluate and develop advanced robotic technologies for manufacturing, logistics, inspection, cleaning, research and service robotics.

hero.title: From Advanced Robots to Real Industrial Applications
hero.subtitle: MegaRobotics is a Germany-based industrial robotics and automation partner focused on evaluating, sourcing and developing robotic solutions for manufacturing, logistics, inspection, cleaning, research and service environments.

whatWeDo.title: Robotics technology, industrial use cases and integration expertise
whatWeDo.body: MegaRobotics connects industrial automation challenges with suitable robotic technologies. We support customers and partners in defining requirements, evaluating platforms, coordinating with manufacturers and developing feasible pilot projects.

cards.title: Application analysis
cards.body: Defining the task, environment, payload, cycle time, safety and integration constraints before hardware selection.

cards.title: Robot / platform evaluation
cards.body: Comparing robot arms, mobile platforms, end effectors and perception across the relevant suppliers.

cards.title: Manufacturer coordination
cards.body: Structuring technical and commercial discussions with robotics manufacturers and technology providers.

cards.title: Pilot project development
cards.body: Translating evaluations into feasible first deployments — scope, success criteria, timeline, budget.

cards.title: Integration partner matching
cards.body: Connecting customers with system integrators when in-house integration is not the right path.

cards.title: European market development
cards.body: Use-case mapping, customer discovery and partner coordination for robotics manufacturers entering Europe.

robotTechnologies.title: Robot Technologies
robotTechnologies.intro: Industrial automation increasingly combines multiple robot classes. The right choice depends on payload, reach, speed, environment, safety, perception and integration complexity.

items.description: Articulated 6-axis robots for heavy-duty, high-payload and high-reach industrial tasks.

items.title: Collaborative Robots

items.title: Parallel Robots
items.description: Delta and parallel-kinematic systems for high-speed pick-and-place at low payload.

items.title: AMR / AGV / Mobile Robots

items.title: Quadruped / Legged Robots

items.title: Humanoid Robots

forCustomers.title: Find the Right Robot for Your Application
forCustomers.intro: Choosing a robotic system is not only a product decision. It requires understanding the task, environment, safety, payload, perception, integration, service model and commercial feasibility.

sections.title: Application discussion
sections.body: Clarifying the task, environment and constraints before any platform is recommended.

sections.title: Technical requirement check
sections.body: Payload, reach, cycle time, safety, perception, footprint and integration interfaces.

sections.title: Robot / platform comparison

sections.title: Deployment and support path

meta.title: Projects & Concept Studies | MegaRobotics
meta.description: Concept and feasibility studies for automotive aftermarket robotic cells, mobile inspection, intralogistics, research robotics platforms and industrial cleaning automation.

projects.title: Projects & Concept Studies
projects.intro: The projects below describe automation scenarios MegaRobotics develops with customers and integration partners. Items labelled "Concept / Feasibility Study" are scoped frameworks, not deployed references.

items.title: Automotive Body-Shop Robotic Cell
items.subtitle: Robotic sanding, polishing and painting concept for aftermarket environments.
items.body: Force-controlled arm with compliant end-effector, targeted at small- and mid-volume aftermarket body shops. Scope covers cell layout, safety concept, dust extraction interface, and integration with existing paint-prep workflows.

items.title: Industrial Inspection Robot Pilot
items.subtitle: Mobile and legged robot concepts for repetitive inspection and remote monitoring.
items.body: Comparison of indoor AMR and quadruped options for facility patrol routes, thermal inspection, gauge reading and shift-handover reporting. Includes data path to existing maintenance systems.

items.title: Intralogistics Automation Concept
items.subtitle: AMR and mobile robot concepts for material flow and warehouse operations.
items.body: Scenario design for inter-zone transport, goods-to-person stations and unmanned forklift segments. Covers fleet sizing, charging, traffic management and IT integration.

items.title: Research Robotics Platform Evaluation
items.subtitle: Humanoid, quadruped and mobile platforms for universities and robotics labs.
items.body: Platform comparison and procurement support for research groups working on locomotion, manipulation and embodied AI. Includes software-stack maturity and long-term support assessment.

items.title: Industrial Cleaning Automation
items.subtitle: Commercial and industrial cleaning robot deployment concepts.
items.body: Site survey, cleaning-pattern definition and platform selection for large-surface industrial and commercial cleaning, including charging infrastructure and operational hand-over.

meta.title: Company | MegaRobotics
meta.description: MegaRobotics is based in Germany and focuses on industrial robotics, automation technologies and advanced robotic platforms for European industrial, research and service applications.

company.title: About MegaRobotics
company.body: MegaRobotics is based in Germany and focuses on industrial robotics, automation technologies and advanced robotic platforms. The company works across customer discovery, robot sourcing, feasibility development and technology coordination for European industrial, research and service applications.
company.positioning: MegaRobotics helps European industrial customers and research institutions identify, evaluate and develop advanced robotic technologies across manufacturing, logistics, inspection, cleaning, service robotics and embodied AI applications.
company.approach: We start from the application, not the product. Requirements, environment and feasibility are clarified before any platform recommendation. Manufacturer and integrator relationships are confirmed on a per-project basis.

contactExtra.intro: Tell us about your automation task, robotic platform interest or manufacturer cooperation proposal.

meta.title: Automation Components for Robotic Systems | MegaRobotics
meta.description: PLC systems, actuators, drives, sensors, vision, grippers, safety systems and industrial communication technologies for robotic automation projects.

automationComponents.title: Automation Components for Robotic Systems
automationComponents.intro: Robotic systems depend on more than the robot itself. PLCs, actuators, drives, sensors, end-effectors, safety systems and industrial communication define whether a robot can be integrated reliably into a real production environment.
automationComponents.sectionsTitle: Six pillars of an integrated robotic system

sections.title: PLC, IPC & industrial control
sections.body: Programmable logic controllers, industrial PCs, remote I/O and edge controllers — the brains that orchestrate robots, machines, conveyors and safety logic.

sections.title: Actuators, drives & motion
sections.body: Servo motors, drives, linear axes, gear motors, pneumatic and rotary actuators that move every fixture, feeder and auxiliary axis around the robot.

sections.title: Grippers & end-effectors
sections.body: Mechanical, electric, pneumatic, vacuum, adaptive and dexterous tools, plus dispensing, sanding, welding and polishing heads — the part that does the actual work.

sections.title: Sensors, vision & perception
sections.body: 2D and 3D cameras, LiDAR, safety scanners, force / torque sensors, proximity and AI-driven inspection systems — the senses of modern robotic automation.

sections.title: Safety & machine protection
sections.body: Safety scanners, light curtains, safety PLCs, interlocks, cell guarding, emergency-stop systems and risk assessment support for compliant deployments.

sections.title: Communication, HMI & software
sections.body: PROFINET, EtherCAT, EtherNet/IP, OPC UA, IO-Link, MQTT, HMI panels, SCADA, fleet management and digital twin software for production integration.

automationComponents.safeWording: MegaRobotics supports sourcing, evaluation and coordination of automation components for robotic systems. Specific brand availability and distributor status are confirmed individually before any commercial engagement.
automationComponents.ctaBody: Tell us which components you are evaluating — we help structure the technical and commercial discussion.

robot-platforms.title: Robot Platforms
robot-platforms.shortDescription: Industrial arms, cobots, mobile robots, quadruped, humanoid and service platforms.
robot-platforms.intro: Robot platforms are the moving body of an automation system. The right platform depends on task, payload, environment, safety profile and integration complexity.
body: Industrial 6-axis robots, collaborative arms, delta and SCARA robots, AMRs and AGVs, quadruped inspection robots, humanoid platforms and mobile manipulators each address a different class of automation problem.
Selection starts from the application — payload, reach, cycle time, environment and safety drive everything else: gripper, vision, controller and integration choices.

meta.title: Robot Platforms | MegaRobotics
meta.description: Industrial arms, cobots, parallel robots, AMR/AGV, quadruped, humanoid and mobile manipulators — sourced and evaluated for European applications.

end-effectors-robot-tooling.title: End Effectors & Robot Tooling
end-effectors-robot-tooling.shortDescription: Grippers, force sensors, tool changers and application-specific end tooling.
end-effectors-robot-tooling.intro: The end-effector defines what the robot can actually do. Grippers, vacuum tools, force sensors, sanding heads, polishing tools, dispensing systems and tool changers turn a robot platform into an application-specific solution.
body: End-of-arm tooling is often the deciding factor for whether a robotic cell is reliable in production. The choice depends on the part geometry, surface, weight, throughput and the kind of force or compliance required.
Tool changers, force / torque sensors and adaptive grippers enable a single robot to perform multiple tasks across product variants.

meta.title: End Effectors & Robot Tooling | MegaRobotics
meta.description: Grippers, vacuum tools, force/torque sensors, dispensing and finishing tools — the end-of-arm technology that defines what a robotic cell can actually do.

motion-actuators-drives.title: Motion, Actuators & Drives
motion-actuators-drives.shortDescription: Servo motors, drives, linear axes, gear motors and pneumatic actuators around the robot.
motion-actuators-drives.intro: Many automation tasks require dedicated motion beyond the robot itself. Servo motors, drives, linear axes, pneumatic cylinders, gear motors and rotary actuators are used in feeders, fixtures, conveyors, handling modules and robotic workcells.
body: A typical robotic cell contains far more motion than the robot itself: 7th-axis rails, indexers, conveyors, feeders and auxiliary axes. Choosing the right drive technology — servo, stepper, frequency inverter, pneumatic — depends on speed, accuracy, duty cycle and integration with the robot controller.
Gearboxes, harmonic drives and encoders determine the achievable repeatability and stiffness of these auxiliary axes.

meta.title: Motion, Actuators & Drives | MegaRobotics
meta.description: Servo motors, drives, linear axes, pneumatic cylinders, gearboxes and motion controllers for robotic cells and industrial automation.

plc-control-industrial-automation.title: PLC, Control & Industrial Automation
plc-control-industrial-automation.shortDescription: PLCs, IPCs, edge controllers, I/O, HMI and electrical control architecture.
plc-control-industrial-automation.intro: Robotic systems often need to communicate with existing factory control architectures. PLCs, IPCs, I/O systems, HMI panels and industrial networks are essential for integrating robots into production lines, machines and safety systems.
body: The PLC layer remains the backbone of industrial automation. A robot rarely operates in isolation — it is supervised, sequenced and interlocked by a PLC or industrial PC that also handles auxiliary motion, safety logic, HMI and connectivity to MES / ERP systems.
The choice of PLC family typically follows the plant's existing standards (Siemens, Beckhoff, Rockwell, Schneider, Mitsubishi, Omron, B&R) and the required protocol stack.

meta.title: PLC, Control & Industrial Automation | MegaRobotics
meta.description: PLCs, IPCs, edge controllers, robot controllers, I/O systems and HMI panels for integrating robots into industrial control architectures.

sensors-vision-perception.title: Sensors, Vision & Perception
sensors-vision-perception.shortDescription: Cameras, LiDAR, safety scanners and force sensors that let robots see and feel.
sensors-vision-perception.intro: Perception is critical for modern robotic systems. Cameras, LiDAR, safety scanners, proximity sensors, force sensors and AI inspection systems help robots locate parts, navigate environments and verify quality.
body: Modern robotic applications increasingly depend on perception — bin picking, AGV navigation, AI inspection, force-controlled finishing. The right sensor depends on accuracy, frame rate, lighting tolerance and the software ecosystem you can integrate with.
Safety-rated sensors (laser scanners, light curtains) are a separate category and must follow the cell's risk assessment.

meta.title: Sensors, Vision & Perception | MegaRobotics
meta.description: 2D/3D cameras, LiDAR, safety scanners, force sensors and AI inspection systems for robotic automation and industrial perception.

safety-machine-protection.title: Safety & Machine Protection
safety-machine-protection.shortDescription: Safety scanners, light curtains, safety PLCs, interlocks and cell guarding.
safety-machine-protection.intro: Industrial robot projects require safety concepts, protective devices and risk reduction measures. Safety scanners, light curtains, interlocks, safety PLCs, robot cell guarding and emergency stop systems are part of a reliable automation architecture.
body: Functional safety is not optional. The choice of safety scanners, light curtains, safety relays and safety PLCs must follow a documented risk assessment per the relevant standards (e.g. EN ISO 12100, EN ISO 13849, EN ISO 10218 for industrial robots, EN ISO 13482 for service robots).
MegaRobotics coordinates with integration partners and accredited bodies on CE conformity. We do not act as a certification body — the certification itself is performed by accredited assessors.

meta.title: Safety & Machine Protection | MegaRobotics
meta.description: Safety scanners, light curtains, safety PLCs, interlocks and cell guarding for compliant industrial robot deployments.

industrial-communication-connectivity.title: Industrial Communication & Connectivity
industrial-communication-connectivity.shortDescription: Field networks, gateways and edge connectivity for robotic systems.
industrial-communication-connectivity.intro: Industrial communication protocols define how robots, PLCs, sensors and IT systems exchange data. Reliable Ethernet-based field networks and edge-to-cloud connectivity are essential for modern automation.
body: PROFINET, EtherCAT, EtherNet/IP, Modbus TCP, IO-Link and OPC UA cover most of the modern industrial communication landscape. The choice depends on the plant standard, the robot controller and the integration with MES / ERP systems.
Edge connectivity (industrial routers, MQTT, OPC UA, 5G modules) enables remote monitoring, predictive maintenance and integration with cloud and IoT platforms.

meta.title: Industrial Communication & Connectivity | MegaRobotics
meta.description: PROFINET, EtherCAT, EtherNet/IP, IO-Link, OPC UA and MQTT for connecting robots, PLCs, sensors and IT systems.

software-hmi-scada-digital-twin.title: Software, HMI, SCADA & Digital Twin
software-hmi-scada-digital-twin.shortDescription: Robot programming, simulation, HMI, SCADA, fleet management and digital twin software.
software-hmi-scada-digital-twin.intro: Robot programming, simulation, fleet management and operator interfaces — software defines how operators, engineers and IT systems interact with robotic assets.
body: Robot programming environments, offline programming, simulation and digital-twin tools shorten commissioning time and let engineers validate cells before hardware exists.
Production-side software — HMI, SCADA, AMR fleet management, AI inspection software, predictive maintenance — closes the loop between robots and the rest of the plant.

meta.title: Software, HMI, SCADA & Digital Twin | MegaRobotics
meta.description: Robot programming, simulation, digital twin, HMI, SCADA, fleet management and AI inspection software for robotic and industrial automation.

robotic-cells-application-packages.title: Robotic Cells & Application Packages
robotic-cells-application-packages.shortDescription: Pre-engineered robotic cells for palletizing, machine tending, inspection and finishing.
robotic-cells-application-packages.intro: Pre-engineered robotic cells combine robot platforms, end-effectors, vision, safety and software into application-specific solutions for handling, palletizing, packaging, inspection and finishing tasks.
body: Application packages reduce engineering risk and shorten time-to-production. They package a robot, end-effector, safety concept and software for a defined task — palletizing, machine tending, pick-and-place, surface finishing or inspection.
MegaRobotics coordinates with integration partners on cell engineering, commissioning and operational hand-over.

meta.title: Robotic Cells & Application Packages | MegaRobotics
meta.description: Pre-engineered robotic cells for palletizing, machine tending, picking, packaging, inspection and surface finishing.

service-cleaning-facility-robots.title: Service, Cleaning & Facility Robots
service-cleaning-facility-robots.shortDescription: Cleaning, delivery, security and facility robots for commercial environments.
service-cleaning-facility-robots.intro: Service robots automate recurring facility tasks such as cleaning, delivery, security patrol and reception. Suited for commercial, retail, hospitality and industrial environments.
body: Service and cleaning robots are increasingly deployed in commercial environments — large-surface scrubbers, autonomous delivery, security patrol and reception robots that complement human staff.
Deployment success depends as much on operational fit (charging, scheduling, hand-over) as on the robot itself.

meta.title: Service, Cleaning & Facility Robots | MegaRobotics
meta.description: Autonomous cleaning, delivery, security patrol and facility-service robots for commercial and industrial environments.

research-education-embodied-ai.title: Research, Education & Embodied AI
research-education-embodied-ai.shortDescription: Humanoid, quadruped, mobile manipulation and teleoperation platforms for labs.
research-education-embodied-ai.intro: Research-grade platforms for universities, applied-research labs and embodied-AI groups. Humanoid, quadruped, mobile manipulation and teleoperation platforms used to study locomotion, manipulation and learning.
body: Research robotics has its own selection criteria: openness of the platform, software-stack maturity, sensor access, simulation parity and long-term support.
MegaRobotics supports procurement, evaluation and platform comparison for research groups and teaching labs.

meta.title: Research, Education & Embodied AI | MegaRobotics
meta.description: Humanoid, quadruped, mobile manipulation and teleoperation platforms for university research and embodied AI labs.

spare-parts-modules-accessories.title: Spare Parts, Modules & Accessories
spare-parts-modules-accessories.shortDescription: Robot joints, batteries, controllers, end-effector accessories and mounting kits.
spare-parts-modules-accessories.intro: Modular components and spares — robot joints, batteries, controllers, end-effector accessories and mounting kits — for maintaining and extending existing robotic deployments.
body: Long-term operation of a robotic deployment depends on the availability of spares and modular components: joints, batteries, chargers, controllers, payload mounts and cabling.
MegaRobotics supports sourcing of spares and modular components to keep deployed systems in service.

meta.title: Spare Parts, Modules & Accessories | MegaRobotics
meta.description: Robot joints, batteries, chargers, controllers, cables, mounting plates and end-effector accessories for ongoing robotic deployments.

productDetail.distributorNotice: Product availability, specifications, pricing and distributor status may vary by region and project. Please contact MegaRobotics for current sourcing and commercial information.
